panels.description: Medium shot: Clerval warmly greeting Frankenstein on a 19th-century cobblestone street. Frankenstein looks visibly moved and teary-eyed, his posture relaxing as he sees his friend.

bubbles.text: My dear Frankenstein, how glad I am to see you! How fortunate that you should be here at the very moment of my alighting!
Seeing Clerval brings back all the dear memories of home—my father, Elizabeth...

panels.description: Close up: Frankenstein firmly grasping Clerval's hand. Focus on their linked hands and Frankenstein's face showing sudden relief and calm.

bubbles.text: For the first time in months, I felt a sense of calm and serene joy.

panels.description: Medium close-up: Clerval walking with Frankenstein, talking animatedly, gesturing with his hands. Clerval looks cheerful, Frankenstein listens intently.

bubbles.text: It was difficult to convince my father that life wasn't just about book-keeping. He'd say: 'I have ten thousand florins a year without Greek!'

panels.description: Medium shot: Clerval looking critically at Frankenstein's face. Clerval appears concerned, while Frankenstein looks down, pale and tired.

bubbles.text: My dear Frankenstein, I didn't notice before... you look so thin and pale. Have you been watching for nights?

panels.description: Low angle: The exterior of the dark, imposing college building. Frankenstein and Clerval are small silhouettes approaching the gate.

bubbles.text: I trembled, unable to think of the horror I left in my room.

panels.description: Medium shot: Frankenstein standing before his apartment door, hand trembling on the lock, eyes wide with fear and anticipation.

bubbles.text: Please, Henry, stay here... just for a moment.
